title: World In 5 | Jul 01 — Police units deployed across South Africa before a
description: Riots, earthquakes, and a president's crypto windfall - it's a wild day in global news, and we're just getting started. In South Africa, police units are flooding the streets ahead of planned anti-immigration marches, and the government is bracing for the worst. You might remember the devastating anti-migrant violence back in 2008, which left 62 people dead and a trail of destruction in its wake. Authorities are determined to avoid a repeat of that chaos, deploying security personnel to key area...

title: World In 5 | Jun 30 — Police units deployed across South Africa before a
description: Racial tensions are simmering in South Africa, where police units have been deployed across the country ahead of anti-immigration marches, sparking fears of a repeat of the deadly violence that erupted in 2008. The authorities are taking no chances, with security personnel patrolling the streets of major cities like Johannesburg, where many shopkeepers have chosen to stay closed today. title: World In 5 | Jun 30 — Police units deployed across South Africa before a
description: Tensions are running high in South Africa today as police units have been deployed across the country in anticipation of anti-immigration marches, sparking fears of a repeat of the deadly violence that rocked the nation back in 2008. title: World News Jun 29 — Pakistani strikes kill dozens in Afghanistan
description: Dozens of civilians are dead and many more are injured in Afghanistan after Pakistani airstrikes ripped through the border region, sparking outrage and escalating tensions between the two nations. The Taliban government claims that the strikes targeted innocent people, including women and children, while Pakistan insists that it was targeting militant groups.
